note: Manual layer of dashboard/portfolio-hq.html: everything here comes from live PostHog reads, founder statements, or weekly readouts — not derivable from status.json. Update this file (and bump asOf), then run ./agentic-os refresh — it regenerates portfolio-hq.html as its last step.

founderCommand.title: Land Resumely Release A on main — the shipped build is not on main
founderCommand.body: Founder submitted 1.4.2 (12) to ASC on 2026-07-15, built from branch codex/first-time-journey-release-a. origin/main does not contain Stories 1-6, the FTUX plan docs, or the 1.4.2 version bump. If review passes, the live App Store build will have no corresponding commit on main — main cannot rebuild what users are running. Resumely iOS tasks/progress.md still reads 'ASC archive attempt BLOCKED (2026-07-15)' and contradicts the founder's submission statement.
founderCommand.prompt: In the Resumely iOS repo, open a PR from codex/first-time-journey-release-a into main covering Release A Stories 1-6 and the 1.4.2 (12) version bump. Preserve unrelated dirty files. Run the focused suites plus Debug and generic-device Release builds before merging. Then correct tasks/progress.md: per founder confirmation the 1.4.2 (12) archive and ASC submission did occur on 2026-07-15.

founderCommand.title: Start Resumely FTUX Release B (Stories 7-10)
founderCommand.body: Founder approved B then C as one FTUX push on 2026-07-15. The gated initiation prompt already exists at docs/specs/drafts/release-b-initiation-prompt.md (preserved on PR #96). Story 9 stays hard-blocked until the backend recommendation-evidence metadata contract has a named owner, schema, delivery plan and fallback.
founderCommand.prompt: In the Resumely iOS repo, follow docs/specs/drafts/release-b-initiation-prompt.md. Implement Stories 7-10 in order: preserve guest context through auth, merge Fit into the diagnosis continuation, evidence-backed Accept/Skip review, canonical activation instrumentation. Do not begin Story 9 until the backend evidence-metadata contract is recorded and approved. Branch from the Release A merge point once it lands on main.

founderCommand.title: Merge the FTUX artifact rescue (PR #96)
founderCommand.body: The Resumely first-time-user journey audit, its 20 evidence screenshots, the Figma contact sheet, the Release A ASC readiness report and the Release B initiation prompt were untracked in every branch — they existed only on one local disk. They are now committed and pushed on docs/ftux-audit-rescue, but the PR is still open.
founderCommand.prompt: Review and merge ResumeBuilder-IOS-APP PR #96 (docs-only artifact rescue). Confirm nothing else in the Resumely or RunSmart repos is carrying untracked artifacts worth preserving.

tracks.name: Portfolio Activation Playbook v2
tracks.standing: The canonical activation contract remains Intent → Setup → First value → Verified value → Repeat value. Founder-excluded mature D7 snapshots drive decisions. Both products' FTUX work is now submitted to Apple and awaiting review — the next activation read is the first that can show whether FTUX moved anything.

items.text: Primary metrics locked: RunSmart physical install → run_completed within D7; Resumely first seen → export_success within D7
Both FTUX releases submitted to ASC 2026-07-15: RunSmart 1.0.9 (23) and Resumely 1.4.2 (12). Neither is live yet
Resumely Releases B then C approved as one FTUX push (founder, 2026-07-15); Release B Stories 7-10 are the active lane
Founder is actively developing Playbook V2 and the distribution ranking with Claude Code; preserve that work as the current planning lane
WP-45 S0 measurement contract and baseline implemented on Resumely iOS branch codex/wp45-s0-measurement-contract at d53d091; S1 is next after the branch is pushed and reviewed
Cumulative clean Resumely export remains under review because the manual one-export claim conflicts with the saved clean report
Next gates: five observed Resumely sessions, July 18 minimum cohort read, July 25 definitive read, August 1 CEO/COO decision

tracks.name: RunSmart FTUX upgrade
tracks.standing: 1.0.9 (23) was archived and submitted to App Store Connect on 2026-07-15 (RunSmart iOS main commit 6cb4094, PR #94); the founder-device S6/S1 smoke was waived at submission. The live App Store build is still 1.0.8 (22) until review clears. WP-43, WP-44 and WP-45 are all merged to main and the full suite passes 273 tests.

items.text: WP-43 P0 trust hardening merged: plan-generation state, human auth errors, consistent timeline, interval parsing, user-facing source labels and explicit onboarding choices
WP-44 S1-S6 implemented: first-screen promise, async failure states, single-source metrics, onboarding polish, rest-day guidance and in-app Terms/Privacy
WP-45 instrumentation remainder implemented, including abandonment, permissions, HealthKit failure, first-workout, report and insight events
PR #89 and release prep PR #90 merged to main; 1.0.9 (23) simulator smoke caught and fixed two numeric consistency bugs
1.0.9 (23) archived and submitted to ASC 2026-07-15 (commit 6cb4094, PR #94); zero-streak profile fix (PR #92) landed first
Apple review outcome. The S6 empty-goal and S1 plan-generation failure states were never smoked on a physical device — that gap shipped with the build
Experiments E1-E3 start only after the release is live and the new PostHog events are verified

tracks.name: Resumely FTUX upgrade
tracks.standing: Founder archived and submitted Release A as 1.4.2 (12) to ASC on 2026-07-15. The build was cut from codex/first-time-journey-release-a — Stories 1-6 and the version bump are NOT on origin/main, so main cannot currently rebuild the submitted binary. Founder approved Releases B then C as one FTUX push on 2026-07-15.

items.text: Release A: golden-path harness, deterministic Apply route, one optimization source, shared job validation, recommendation safety and preview/save/export/relaunch recovery
1.4.2 (12) archived and submitted to ASC 2026-07-15 (founder-confirmed). Live App Store build stays 1.4.1 (11) until review clears
Land Release A on main — the shipped code lives only on codex/first-time-journey-release-a, and progress.md still says the archive was BLOCKED
Release B Stories 7-10 approved and next: preserve guest context through auth, merge Fit into diagnosis, evidence-backed Accept/Skip review, canonical activation instrumentation
Story 9 is hard-blocked until the backend recommendation-evidence metadata contract has an owner, schema, delivery plan and fallback (open question Q1)
Release C Stories 11-13 queued behind B: touched-screen localization/accessibility, optimize-another-job retention loop, release-candidate journey audit

tracks.name: RunSmart WP-40 / Apple Health activation
tracks.standing: RunSmart 1.0.8 (22) is live on App Store Connect, founder-confirmed 2026-07-13. WP-40 S1+S2 merged to RunSmart main as 236dde0 via PR #84; S3 physical-device evidence and S4's post-merge read are complete. WP-42 still has zero clean disclosure viewers after exclusions, so no HealthKit product-change verdict is valid yet.

items.text: Skippable Apple Health onboarding step added (OnboardingView.swift), reusing the existing HealthKit connection route and analytics; Connect/Skip accessibility identifiers wired

TDD red state reproduced then final focused test passed (1/1); debug simulator build passed; Health prompt appeared during onboarding; Skip advanced to Ready; Connect followed the real HealthKit path; git diff --check passed; only known warning is a pre-existing deprecated HKWorkout initializer
S1–S4 closeout and the 1.0.8 (22) release handoff are preserved in RunSmart commit d9ce8fa; founder confirmed the build live on 2026-07-13
Re-run WP-42 after at least one clean production disclosure viewer exists; wait for 10 clean viewers before recommending a product change

tracks.name: Resumely web export — observability fix
tracks.standing: The web export-observability fix merged in PR #114 on 2026-07-11. Core CI, GitGuardian, CodeRabbit and Vercel checks passed; the known Cloudflare Workers check failed independently of the Vercel production path. The export mechanism was already QA-verified end to end. The next valid read is after five real founder/QA-excluded web completers pass through the newly instrumented export path.

items.text: Optimization page now emits optimized_viewed, export_cta_seen, export_pdf_tapped; download route emits authenticated export_started then exactly one of export_success/export_failed, all tagged with optimization_id, platform=web, source, format, renderer/error code

Regression test failed 3/3 before the fix, passes 3/3 after; targeted + full ESLint clean (11 pre-existing warnings, 0 new errors); production build succeeds; TypeScript clean outside documented pre-existing test errors; smoke test generated a 7,328-byte PDF
PR #114 merged 2026-07-11; Vercel check passed and the instrumented export path is now available for measurement
Re-measure optimized_viewed → export_cta_seen → export_pdf_tapped → export_started → export_success/export_failed after 5 real web completers

tracks.name: Michal / AI Audit Toolkit
tracks.standing: Parked by founder decision 2026-07-09. Michal is migrating from Gemini to OpenAI. We wait until she finishes that move, then set her up on Codex and build 'Michal's OS' — a Codex-based operating layer — instead of the drafted Gemini-Gems path. The audit framing carries over; the Gemini delivery vehicle does not.

items.text: Gemini Track-C prep drafted (4 Gems, voice doc, privacy rules) — framing reusable, delivery vehicle superseded
Blocked on Michal's Gemini → OpenAI migration. No action from us until she's moved.
When she's on OpenAI: set her up on Codex and build Michal's OS; then file the first toolkit template so the pilot feeds the AI Audit Toolkit product.

artifacts.note: Durable artifacts built across Builder OS, Agentic OS and the product repos, so nothing gets lost. `state` is the honest storage truth, not a quality rating: `safe` = committed and pushed to a branch on origin; `branch-only` = pushed but never merged to main, so it is invisible to anyone reading main; `local-only` = exists on one disk and one `git clean` from gone. Verified against origin on 2026-07-15 — re-verify before trusting a link after any branch cleanup.

items.title: First-time-user journey audit (2026-07-13)
items.what: The fresh-install walkthrough that produced the entire 13-story FTUX plan. Verdict: strong guest-first positioning, then repeated Analyze/Check Fit work, recommendation-credibility defects, and a critical post-Apply blank/locked state.
items.stateNote: Was local-only until 2026-07-15; rescued onto docs/ftux-audit-rescue, PR #96 open.

items.title: Journey audit evidence — 20 screenshots
items.what: The captured fresh-install journey, screen by screen, from first home through the blank post-Apply state and the mixed-language Hebrew home.

items.title: 13-story FTUX plan (Releases A/B/C)
items.what: The approval-ready story plan. Release A trustworthy completion, Release B continuous evidence-backed journey, Release C reach and retention polish.
items.stateNote: Never merged to main — lives only on codex/first-time-journey-release-a.

items.title: FTUX product brief + feature spec
items.what: The brief and spec behind the story plan — the reasoning layer under the 13 stories.

items.title: Release B initiation prompt (gated)
items.what: The ready-to-paste prompt that starts Stories 7-10, including the Story 9 backend-evidence decision gate. This is the next session's entry point.

items.title: Release A ASC readiness report (2026-07-15)
items.what: The pre-submission readiness assessment. Records the signing-identity blocker that stopped the first archive attempt, before the founder completed the 1.4.2 submission.

items.title: First-time-user journey audit (2026-07-13)
items.what: The RunSmart fresh-install audit. Source of the WP-43/44/45 risk list: blank plan-generation wait, raw Apple sign-in errors, the '21000 x 400 m' workout fabrication, developer-vocabulary badges, and the hidden default goal.
items.stateNote: Pushed to origin but never merged to main; readable only via the audit branch or its worktree.

items.title: FTUX upgrade plan — WP-43/44/45 + experiments
items.what: The plan derived from the audit: P0 trust hardening, first-screen promise and failure states, instrumentation, and experiments E1-E3.

items.title: FTUX audit evidence assets
items.what: Captured screens behind the audit's findings — first screen, health prompt, today/plan, run recording and paused states.

items.title: DECISIONS.md — durable cross-project decisions
items.what: The only durable record of decisions that span products. If a decision is not here, it does not exist.

items.title: PROJECT-STATUS.md — parsed cross-repo status
items.what: Status rebuilt from each repo's task files on every refresh, with a source-confidence rating per project. The anti-drift layer.

items.title: Portfolio HQ manual layer
items.what: The file behind this page. Everything not derivable from status.json — PostHog reads, founder statements, the queue, and this artifact registry.

items.title: Executive dashboard + COO review
items.what: The executive OS layer: board view, executive backlog and the latest COO review.

items.title: Distribution OS — command center + experiment log
items.what: Channel backlog, experiment log and metrics for distribution work, including the WP-32 inconclusive close.

items.title: Token + cost observability
items.what: The usage collector behind the cost panel on the Numbers tab. Bins spend by launch directory.

items.title: wiki-index — catalog of every vault page
items.what: Read first when searching the vault. The curated catalog plus Dataview-generated index health.

items.title: Builder OS Brain Map
items.what: The Excalidraw brain map of the vault — 123 notes, 592 links, regenerated without sync-duplicate phantom nodes.

items.title: Decision Log
items.what: The vault's readable decision index. Durable decisions must also land in Agentic OS DECISIONS.md.

items.title: Decision: priority reset — Resumely primary (2026-07-02)
items.what: The standing decision that makes Resumely primary and puts RunSmart Garmin work in maintenance. Governs what the queue above is allowed to prioritize.

suggestedSessions.model: GPT-5.6 Sol / Codex - tool-heavy verification across repository evidence and build validation
suggestedSessions.title: Land Resumely Release A on main
suggestedSessions.why: 1.4.2 (12) was submitted to Apple from codex/first-time-journey-release-a. Nothing on main can rebuild the binary users are about to receive. This is the highest-value hour in the portfolio right now — it is cheap, and it stops a live build from becoming unreproducible.
suggestedSessions.prompt: Open a PR from Resumely codex/first-time-journey-release-a into main covering Release A Stories 1-6 and the 1.4.2 (12) bump. Preserve unrelated dirty files. Run focused suites plus Debug and generic-device Release builds before merging. Then correct tasks/progress.md, which still claims the ASC archive was BLOCKED.

suggestedSessions.model: GPT-5.6 Sol / Codex - long multi-story implementation against a written plan
suggestedSessions.title: Start FTUX Release B — Stories 7-10
suggestedSessions.why: Founder approved B then C as one push on 2026-07-15. Release B is the half of the FTUX plan that actually attacks the measured D7 wall: guest context loss at auth, duplicated Fit work, and the missing canonical activation instrumentation.
suggestedSessions.prompt: Follow docs/specs/drafts/release-b-initiation-prompt.md in the Resumely iOS repo. Implement Stories 7-10 in order. Do not begin Story 9 until the backend recommendation-evidence metadata contract has a named owner, schema, delivery plan and fallback. Branch from the Release A merge point.

suggestedSessions.title: Merge the artifact rescue and un-strand the FTUX branches
suggestedSessions.why: Both first-time-user journey audits — the documents that justify every FTUX story — exist only on unmerged branches. The Resumely one was untracked entirely until 2026-07-15.
suggestedSessions.prompt: Merge ResumeBuilder-IOS-APP PR #96. Then decide whether the RunSmart FTUX audit and plan on claude/runsmart-ftux-audit-240648 should land on main, and open that PR if so. Re-verify every artifact link on the Portfolio HQ Artifacts tab afterwards.

growth.note: WP-32 closed inconclusive on 2026-07-12 with 3 reactions, 1 comment, no qualitative product feedback, and no available ASC lift read. WP-31 is the next approved draft-only organic asset; publishing remains founder-gated.

traffic.label: Resumely — people opening the app per week (founder excluded)
traffic.story: The launch push worked (26 people, most tried a fit check), then traffic died. This is the number WP-31/32 have to move.

traffic.label: RunSmart — installs per week
traffic.story: Steadier than Resumely, but 0% of installers ever complete a run — volume isn't the problem here, the plan→run step is.

funnels.name: Resumely
funnels.tag: web + iOS · mature D7 cohort through 2026-07-12
funnels.story: Updated 07-12 from live PostHog project 270848: 73 mature founder/test-excluded first-seen people, 9 uploaded within D7, 0 completed an optimization within D7, and 0 exported within D7. The activation gate remains closed. The cumulative clean-export count is under review: the manual one-export claim conflicts with the saved clean report and must not be cited until a reproducible query resolves it.

funnels.tag: iOS · mature organic D7 cohort through 2026-07-12

rows.resumely: first seen→upload→optimization — 73 first seen, 9 uploaded, 0 optimized within D7
rows.runsmart: install→onboarding and plan→run — 13 installed, 1 onboarded/planned, 0 ran

activationHeadline.note: Live read completed 2026-07-12 08:46-09:00 IDT against PostHog UTC projects 171597 and 270848. RunSmart excludes emulator, TestFlight, sideloaded and known founder prefixes; Resumely excludes is_internal_tester plus the four established founder/QA/bot person prefixes. No monetization, paid ads, or product-change claim is unlocked by these 0% results. Re-read Resumely's post-live 1.4.1 picker cohort on 2026-07-18 minimum / 2026-07-25 preferred; re-read WP-42 after one clean disclosure viewer, with 10 required for a product recommendation.

posthogDashboards.decisionSnapshot: 0% mature D7 activation, 0 run completions among 13 eligible installs
posthogDashboards.exclusions: Founder prefixes, QA, emulator, TestFlight and sideloaded users
posthogDashboards.warning: The linked operational dashboard uses rolling 30-day charts, independent event totals and broader traffic. It can include founder signals and is not the mature ordered D7 cohort.

posthogDashboards.exclusions: is_internal_tester plus established founder, QA and bot person prefixes
posthogDashboards.warning: The linked dashboard uses a rolling clean iOS 60-day status view and an export diagnostic with different event filters. Founder exclusion and cohort maturity explain much of the difference.

clocks.what: Both FTUX releases submitted to ASC — RunSmart 1.0.9 (23) and Resumely 1.4.2 (12); founder will confirm approval/live
ASC review outcome for both apps. Typical review is 24-48h; a rejection on either restarts the FTUX release lane
Verify the new WP-43/45 and Release A PostHog events actually fire in production before any FTUX activation claim
Activation re-read complete — Resumely 0/73, RunSmart 0/13; gates remain closed
Resumely 1.4.1 minimum cohort gate — ordered WP-41 re-read; no funnel-only recommendation below 10 eligible users
EXD-017 reminder — did you schedule/hold the RunSmart accountant call? Capture recommendation in WP-35
EXD-015 review — did Resumely hit 20% real activation in 30 days?
WP-32 FB groups closed inconclusive — 3 reactions, 1 comment, ASC lift unavailable
Michal — set up on Codex, build Michal's OS (parked pending her Gemini→OpenAI migration, decided 2026-07-09)

kk: Both FTUX releases were submitted to ASC on 2026-07-15 and are awaiting review (founder-confirmed): RunSmart 1.0.9 (23) and Resumely 1.4.2 (12). Neither is live yet — the live builds remain RunSmart 1.0.8 (22) and Resumely 1.4.1 (11).

Resumely's submitted 1.4.2 (12) was built from codex/first-time-journey-release-a, not main. origin/main has neither Stories 1-6 nor the version bump, so main cannot rebuild the submitted binary until that PR lands.
Both remain at 0% real D7 activation on the 2026-07-12 mature read: Resumely 0/73; RunSmart 0/13.

Resumely D7 wall = first seen→upload→optimization: 73 first seen, 9 uploaded, 0 optimized, 0 exported. PR #114 web export observability is merged; wait for 5 real web completers before reading that new path.
RunSmart wall = install→onboarding and plan→run: 13 mature installs, 1 onboarded/planned, 0 ran. WP-40 S1+S2 merged via PR #84; WP-42 has no clean production disclosure cohort yet.
Traffic collapsed post-launch-push; WP-29 web funnel fixed + verified.
One backend tester account owned 22/23 of all Resumely Supabase optimizations pre-hardening — analytics identity/alias wiring shipped 07-07 (Story 1) to fix this going forward.

Garmin: brand attribution + Internal-Test-vs-Commercial tiering are well-grounded in Garmin's public docs (matches 3 real rejections). Fee structure for RunSmart specifically and the Gate 1-4 checklist are founder-confirmed directly with Marc via email/attachments (2026-07-08) — the 07-06 research pass's "unconfirmed" flag was based on Garmin's generic public docs, not RunSmart's actual terms with Marc; no further written confirmation needed.
ku: Can the next Hebrew distribution test produce a measurable answer after WP-32 closed without attribution? It must use unique campaign links and verified analytics access before publishing.
Did Resumely Stories 2-4 (PR #90, merged 07-08) actually move the upload-picker friction numbers for real (non-founder) traffic? Re-read once enough post-merge volume exists.
Does the merged web export-observability path reveal real web export failures after 5 real completers, or does it confirm the button works for organic users?
Why does no runner start after a plan? The 2026-07-12 read still shows 1 of 13 mature installers reaching onboarding/plan and 0 starting a run; WP-40's clean production cohort is not readable yet.
Is Resumely's cumulative clean export count 0 or 1? The saved clean July 6 report says 0 while a later manual dashboard claim says 1; resolve with a reproducible query before citing it.
Voice-coach flag: DECIDED 2026-07-09 — deferred (stays off), review at the 07-12 activation re-read or after WP-40 lands. No longer an open question.
uk: Michal Track C fully drafted — blocked only on her live Gemini account.

WP files lag Codex/Cursor sessions — dashboards lie until packets are updated. Three Codex/Cursor sessions found work sitting uncommitted or on detached HEAD this week (ResumeBuilder iOS APP x2, RunSmart Web) 07-06/07/08 — branched, verified, and preserved each time (PR #89, #90, #116), but a recurring risk worth remembering before trusting 'no local changes.' WP-32/33/34 were the exception this round — Cursor updated and pushed the packets itself (commit fe9ad95).

status.json's needsDecision still lists "RunSmart iOS: if build 8 is rejected again" — stale, from before 1.0.7(21) shipped and passed review. Not a portfolio-hq bug; RunSmart iOS's own task files need a refresh to clear it. Same staleness pattern flagged by ./agentic-os verify for COO-LATEST-REVIEW.md (references build 18, current is build 21).
ResumeBuilder IOS APP is missing tasks/ERRORS.md and docs/agent-os/project-context.md — flagged as a required-read gap by two separate Codex sessions now.

Artifacts strand silently. Both FTUX audits — the evidence base for all 13 Resumely stories and all three RunSmart work packets — sit on branches never merged to main, and the Resumely one was untracked in every branch until 2026-07-15 (rescued via PR #96). The Artifacts tab now lists each one with its real storage state; treat `branch-only` as one branch-cleanup away from invisible.

Resumely iOS tasks/progress.md contradicts the founder on ASC state: it says the 1.4.2 archive was BLOCKED on signing identity, while the founder confirms the submission happened the same day. Apple-state changes leave no commit, so the repo record cannot self-correct — this is the same staleness pattern that made progress.md read 'in review' when 1.2 (7) was already live.
RunSmart iOS has ~30 untracked Finder-duplicate '* 2.swift'/'* 2.md' files cluttering the repo — pre-existing, same issue already named in status.json's blockers as blocking clean archives.
uu: Does the PostHog leak run both ways (Resumely → RunSmart project)?
Do other dashboards carry founder/QA contamination (the 50% inflation pattern)?
Is 'activation' the right north star before there's traffic to activate?

weekPriorities.title: Resumely — land Release A on main
weekPriorities.body: 1.4.2 (12) went to Apple from a feature branch. Until that PR lands, the build users receive has no commit on main behind it. Cheap to fix now, expensive after the next branch cleanup.

weekPriorities.title: Resumely — start Release B (Stories 7-10)
weekPriorities.body: Founder approved B then C as one FTUX push. Release B is the part that attacks the measured D7 wall: guest context through auth, merged Fit, evidence-backed review, canonical activation instrumentation. Story 9 stays gated on the backend evidence contract.

weekPriorities.title: FTUX measurement — preserve the D7 contract
weekPriorities.body: Use Portfolio HQ's founder-excluded mature D7 cohort for decisions. Use the authenticated PostHog links for operational drill-down, not as a like-for-like replacement.

weekPriorities.title: OS hygiene — keep the artifacts findable
weekPriorities.body: The Artifacts tab is now the index of everything built across Builder OS, Agentic OS and the product repos. Merge PR #96, decide whether the RunSmart audit branch lands on main, and correct Resumely's progress.md so the repo stops contradicting the founder on ASC state.

readouts.wall: 73 first seen → 9 uploaded → 0 optimized/exported within D7
